evals.name: semicolon positive classic model
evals.prompt:
Assess this strong public-domain source-model paraphrase.

<example>A public-domain source model from Federalist No. 10: semicolons keep related political clauses in sequence</example>
evals.expected_output: The response names the skill concept, preserves a strong classic model, and judges it by objective rubric checks.
assertions: The output identifies the relevant Oiticica concept as oiticica-semicolon.
The output uses Principle, Preserve, Why, and Rubric sections, and does not use Weak, Fault, or Better as repair headings.
The Rubric applies at least two objective checks from the skill, with pass or fail judgments.

evals.name: semicolon negative classic contrast
evals.prompt:
Review this invented weak passage.

<example>The moon rose; the soup was cold; sincerity is rare</example>
evals.expected_output: The response gives a concrete Oiticica contrast and fixes the named fault.
assertions: The output identifies the relevant Oiticica concept as oiticica-semicolon.
The output includes Weak, Fault, Better, Why, and Rubric sections, with the supplied example text in Weak and a Better section that repairs the fault.
The output names a concrete fault in relation, sequence, diction, syntax, sound, or reading rather than saying only unclear, awkward, vague, or verbose.
